text: Amarienborg:
It's the residence of the Danish royal family.
Frederiks Kirke
This is a remarkable example of modern architecture.
from Tuesday to Friday.
The Little Mermaid Statue
This is a character from Grimm's Fairy Tales.
Noma
This world-famous restaurant offers a culinary masterpiece
Rundetaarn
This 17th-century Astronomy tower is a must-visit.
Christiansborg Palace
This is one of the most significant landmarks in Copenhagen, both historically and politically.
from Tuesday to Sunday.
The type of power sockets used in Denmark are Type A and Type K. So, you might need to bring an adapter.
The official currency in Denmark is the Danish Krone (DKK). Most places do not accept euros, so it's best to have some Danish Krone on hand.
There are in total 6 metros in Copenhagen. You can reach anywhere by metro, making it a very convenient mode of transportation.
The weather in Copenhagen in December can be quite cold, with temperatures often dropping below -15 degrees Celsius. Be sure to pack warm clothes.
The official language is Danish, but English is widely spoken in Copenhagen, so you should have no problem communicating.
Copenhagen follows left-hand drive. If you plan on renting a car, this is good to know.
